message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    In the absence of a consensus protocol to slow down the current SARS-CoV2 spread, policy makers are in need of real-time indicators to support decisions in public health matters. The Basic Reproduction Number (
                    <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                    <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                    ) represents viral spread rate and can be dramatically modified by the application of effective public control measures. However, current methodologies to calculate
                    <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                    <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                    from data remain cumbersome and unusable during an outbreak.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Objective</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    To provide a simple mathematical formulation for obtaining
                    <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                    <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                    in Real-Time, and apply it to assess the effectiveness of public-health policies in different iconic countries.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Study design</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    By modifying the equations describing the spread of the virus, we derived a real-time
                    <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                    <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                    estimator that can be readily calculated from daily official case reports.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    We show the application of a time trend analysis of the
                    <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                    <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                    estimator to assess the efficacy and promptness of public health measures that impacted on the development of the COVID-19 epidemic in iconic countries.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusions</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>We propose our simple estimator and method as useful tools to follow and assess in real time the effectiveness of public health policies on COVID-19 evolution.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Real-time estimation of R 0 for supporting public-health policies against COVID-19

reference.unstructured: Breban, R. , Vardavas, R. , Blower, S. , 2007. Theory versus data: how to calculate r0? PLoS One 2.

reference.article-title: A modified sir model for the covid-19 contagion in italy
A time-dependent sir model for covid-19
A contribution to the mathematical theory of epidemics. Proceedings of the royal society of london
reference.journal-title: Series A, Containing papers of a mathematical and physical character

reference.unstructured: MINSAL, 2020a. Official numbers for the coronavirus outbreak in chile. https://www.gob.cl/coronavirus/cifrasoficiales/. accessed: 2020-04-23.
MINSAL, 2020b. Reporte Coronavirus 23/04/2020. Technical Report. Ministerio de Salud, Chile.

reference.article-title: An introduction to the basic reproduction number in mathematical epidemiology
A simple stochastic sir model for covid 19 infection dynamics for karnataka: Learning from europe

reference.unstructured: Worldometers.info, 16 April, 2020. Official numbers for the coronavirus outbreak in chile. https://www.worldometers.info/coronavirus/. accessed: 2020-04-23.

reference.article-title: Modified seir and ai prediction of the epidemics trend of covid-19 in china under public health interventions
